plainEnglish: At Level AAA, give users a way to find the expanded form of abbreviations and acronyms. Expand it on first use, or use the <abbr title="..."> element so the full term is available. This helps people who don't recognize the shorthand.
whyItMatters: Users with cognitive or reading disabilities, and newcomers to a field, can't decode abbreviations like 'ARIA' or 'SLA' unless the expansion is provided.
quickCheck: Confirm abbreviations are expanded on first use or wrapped in <abbr title="full form">.
commonMistakes: Using acronyms with no expansion on first use and no <abbr> markup.
Relying only on the <abbr> title tooltip, which isn't reachable by keyboard or touch — expand it in text too.
Assuming an abbreviation is universally understood.

codeExamples.label: Abbreviation with its expansion available
codeExamples.code: Add an ARIA role.
